opinion.h4: FOX v. UNITED STATES. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE EASTERN DISTRICT OF LOUISIANA. No. 1032. Decided June 1, 1965.
opinion.p: Sidney W. Provensal, Jr., for appellant.
Solicitor General Cox for the United States.
The motion to affirm is granted and the judgment is affirmed.
opinion.h4: MOTOR CONVOY, INC., v. UNITED STATES, (1965)
opinion.p: MOTOR CONVOY, INC., ET AL. v. UNITED STATES ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE NORTHERN DISTRICT OF GEORGIA. No. 987. Decided June 1, 1965.
Paul M. Daniell, Robert E. Born and Martin Sack for appellants.

Solicitor General Cox, Assistant Attorney General Orrick, Robert B. Hummel, Robert W. Ginnane and Betty Jo Christian for the United States et al.; George S. Dixon for National Automobile Transporters Association; and Nelson M. Harris, Jr., for Dixie Transport Co., appellees.
The motions to affirm are granted and the judgment is affirmed.
